conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children - Alex, who's 8, and Blake, who's 9. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (8 years old) and Blake (9 years old).

Let me start by asking: Are you responsible for Alex and Blake? By this, I mean do you live with them and are you responsible for paying for things like their food, clothes, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. They both live with me and I'm responsible for all their needs - food, clothes, school things, everything they need really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask: Are Alex and Blake under 16 years old, or if they're 16 or over, are they in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both under 16 - Alex is 8 and Blake is 9, so yes, they're well under that age.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency: Do you normally live in the UK? This includes England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have a few more questions to ensure I can assess your eligibility accurately.

Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex or Blake, or does anyone else have an equally strong claim to be responsible for them (for example, if they also live with another parent or guardian who provides for them)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. They live with me full-time and I'm the one responsible for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Just to be thorough, I need to ask: Are Alex or Blake currently in care (looked after by the local authority), or are they in hospital long-term?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in care or in hospital. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. One final question: Are you subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on your right to live or claim benefits in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any immigration restrictions. I have the right to live and claim benefits here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now give you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

**For Alex (8 years old):** ✓ Eligible
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Alex as you live together and provide for all their needs
- You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in care or hospital

**For Blake (9 years old):** ✓ Eligible
- Blake is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Blake as you live together and provide for all their needs
- You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- Blake is not in care or hospital

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for both children.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16 years old; (2) You are responsible for Alex as you live with them and pay for their food, clothes, and other needs; (3) You normally live in the UK (Manchester); (4) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (5) Alex is not in care or hospital long-term; (6) You have no immigration restrictions. All the eligibility criteria are met according to the rules at gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility.

Blake is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Blake is 9 years old, which is under 16 years old; (2) You are responsible for Blake as you live with them and pay for their food, clothes, and other needs; (3) You normally live in the UK (Manchester); (4) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (5) Blake is not in care or hospital long-term; (6) You have no immigration restrictions. All the eligibility criteria are met according to the rules at gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children. They are responsible for both Alex (8) and Blake (9), both children are under 16, they live together in the UK, no one else is claiming for the children, neither child is in care or hospital, and the claimant has no immigration restrictions. Both children qualify under the standard Child Benefit eligibility rules.
